question_title: Will the community prediction be higher than 23.00% on 2026-03-12 for the Metaculus question "Will the U.S. enact an AI safety federal statute or executive order in 2026?"?
summary: This meta-prediction asks whether the Metaculus CP on "Will the U.S. enact an AI safety federal statute or executive order in 2026?" will be strictly above 23% on March 12, 2026. The current CP is exactly 23%, and it has not been above 0.23 even once across 108 consecutive readings over the past 6+ weeks. The CP has been range-bound between 0.20-0.23 since mid-January. The Trump administration is explicitly deregulatory on AI, no federal AI safety bills are close to passage, and no near-term catalysts have been identified. With 780 forecasters providing stability, breaking above the 0.23 ceiling would require a meaningful shift. I estimate ~17% probability, leaving room for the irreducible chance of surprise events while heavily weighting the strong empirical pattern.

factors.description: CP has not been strictly above 0.23 even once in 108 data points spanning Feb 1 - Mar 2 (6+ weeks). 0% empirical rate of exceeding threshold.
CP is currently at 0.23, the upper boundary of its recent 0.20-0.23 range. For YES it must reach 0.24+, breaking a long-standing ceiling.
780 forecasters make the CP very stable and resistant to individual forecast changes
Trump administration is explicitly deregulatory on AI; no AI safety EO or federal statute appears imminent. Fundamentals don't support CP moving upward.
No identified catalyst in next 10 days to push forecasters to update upward (no bills near passage, no EO drafts reported)
10 days provides some window for unexpected events (AI incident, surprise policy shift) that could push a few forecasters upward
Slight recent uptick from 0.22 to 0.23 in late Feb could indicate minor upward drift

reasoning: The reflection is approved. Let me now produce the final output.
